page: Amazon Web Services r8id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 7 GHz, providing 8 GB of memory per core. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across various workloads. The processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2194.0 and a multi-core score of 17990.0. It demonstrates high throughput in text and image processing, as well as strong bzip3 decompression speeds. In LLM inference tests, the server achieves a text generation speed of 388.5 tokens per second for small models, though performance drops to 2.78 tokens per second for large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8id.8xlarge offers a high-density memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with fast local NVMe storage, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads without the overhead of external block storage. This resource profile is optimal for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 RAM and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers strong benchmark performance, including top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing scores, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. While it excels at small-scale LLM inference, performance drops significantly on larger models due to the absence of a GPU. With its high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage, the r8id.8xlarge is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, making it an excellent fit for databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 480 MB L3 cache
256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
1900 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and data analytics
